title: Antibody Conjugation and CODEX Multiplexed Immunofluorescence of Fresh Frozen Tissue
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"6q6ir","text":"This protocol describes antibody conjugation, tissue preparation, and microscopy for CODEX multiplexed immunofluorescence analysis of fresh frozen tissue.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"46njd","text":"Wear PPE and work in a well ventilated area.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"cuh6v","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"7uvi7","text":"DMSO readily enters the skin and can carry dissolved chemicals with it.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5ichb","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"fr3ao","text":"Organic solvents and paraformaldehyde are aspiration hazards, and it is recommended that work with them be carried out in a fume hood.  Organic solvents also pose flammability hazards.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5rvj4","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"cjsu4","text":"Chemical handling, storage, and disposal information can be found in Safety Data Sheets (SDS). ACS reagent, ≥99.9%, Sigma-Aldrich, 472301-4L","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"e93j9","text":"Acetone, HPLC Plus, for HPLC, GC, and residue analysis, ≥99.9%, Sigma-Aldrich, 650501-4X4L ","type":"align-left","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9g9sc","text":"Microcentrifuge ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Start with purified antibodies (no BSA, azides, glycerol, etc.). If BSA is present, remove with ab173231.
Block 50 kDa molecular weight cut off filters with 500 µL of filter blocking solution.
Centrifuge at 12000 x g, °C, for 00:02:00 .
Dilute 50 µg of each antibody into 100 µL of PBS and filter with 50 MW cut off filters.
Centrifuge at 12000 x g, °C, for 00:08:00 .

Reduce antibodies with the reduction mixture for 20-25 minutes at ~ 20 °C . 25 minutes of reduction is suitable for many antibodies but may need to be optimized (15 min-40 min) depending on the primary antibody used. In general, shorter reduction times are more appropriate than longer times.
Centrifuge the reduced antibodies at 12000 x g, °C, for 00:08:00 and add buffer solution.

Rehydrate oligonucleotide barcodes in 10 µL nuclease free water and further dilute with 210 µL of conjugation solution. Nuclease free water must be used, otherwise the oligonucleotide barcodes will be degraded.
Respective barcode solutions are added to the reduced primary antibodies and incubated for 02:00:00 at ~ 20 °C
*Purify the solution by buffer exchanging with 450 µL purification solution. Centrifuge at 12000 x g, °C, for 00:08:00 . Discard flow-through.
Remove fresh frozen tissue from -80 °C and place inside a desiccator for 00:20:00 .
Submerge tissues in excess acetone for 00:05:00 .
Rehydrate tissues in hydration buffer for 00:03:00 inside a humidity chamber.
Fix the tissues for 00:10:00 in 1.6% paraformaldehyde in hydration buffer.
Submerge tissues in excess staining buffer for 00:30:00 .

Incubate tissues with a blocking buffer and primary antibody cocktail (1:200 antibody dilution) for 03:00:00 within a humidity chamber at ~ 20 °C . Tissues can be incubated in primary antibody solutions overnight if placed inside a refrigerator. Background may increase if longer incubation times are used but may be necessary for some antigen targets.
Rinse samples with staining buffer and fix with 1.6% paraformaldehyde in storage buffer for 00:10:00 .
Rinse with excess PBS and incubate in cold methanol for 00:05:00 . Rinse samples with excess PBS again.
Fix the samples with fixative solution for 00:10:00 before being stored in storage buffer at 4 °C .

Dilute antibodies in reporter solution to a 1:200 dilution with 1 µL of a 1 mg/mL solution of DAPI or Hoechst 33342. Most secondary oligonucleotide barcode reporters function at a 1:200 dilution; however, this dilution may need to be optimized in select cases or with custom reagents.
Fill respective bottles with nuclease free water, 1x CODEX buffer, and dimethyl sulfoxide.
Mount a blank coverslip into the coverslip microfluidic chamber and flush the fluidic lines.
Mount the prepared tissue sample and incubate with 1x CODEX buffer and 1 µL of 1 mg/mL of DAPI for 00:05:00 and wash with the automated CODEX system.
Set up tiled regions covering the tissue and focus at the tissue surface.

Perform automated microscopy using a Zeiss Axio equipped with a Colibri 7 LED light source and C13440 camera or other equivalent equipment. Important parameters include a 10% tile overlap and a z-stack ranging from 11 to 20 tiles (depending on the tissue size).
go to step #10 twice.
Store in 100 µL antibody storage solution at 4 °C .
creator: Carrie Romer
